run: MANUAL-VERIFICATION / adversarial code review of fix branch
role: reviewer (≠ author — did not write the fix, did not deploy, did not write the fixer's tests)
epistemic_legend: [VERIFIED-WITH-FRESH-EVIDENCE]=produced by this review run, this VM, timestamps below; [INFERRED]=reasoned from code read but not independently executed; [ASSUMED-PENDING-PROBE]=not proven. Prior-phase reports (fixer fix-log.md, qa FINAL-PASS-REPORT.json) are testimony only — every check below was independently re-executed.

checks.method: Two independent git worktrees created (wt-before @ f491170414ef39e3f8ba4ca7106ad6f39c3ec3e8, wt-after @ 9e7befb0367baa5b45aaa977f80028f5c3a15a16); repo-root .env copied into each (gitignored, required for conftest's DSN loader, per EXIT-G06-FINAL-serialized.md convention), removed after; ran via the repo's sanctioned scripts/run-tests.sh (never sourced .env wholesale, DSN schema=aether_test guard enforced by the script itself), under flock /tmp/aether-pytest.lock.

fail_before.setup: Copied the POST-fix test file (98 cases) from wt-after onto wt-before's (pre-fix) code, to reproduce the exact same fail-before scenario the fixer describes
fail_before.command: flock /tmp/aether-pytest.lock bash scripts/run-tests.sh -q -p no:xdist -o addopts="" tests/test_cover006_camelcase.py
fail_before.failed_tests_match: All 11 failures are exactly the 5 named non-Latin gluings + 5 reverse-order variants + 1 end-to-end keyword-panel repro — matches fixer's claimed 11/87 exactly
fail_before.cleanup: git checkout -- apps/api/tests/test_cover006_camelcase.py in wt-before to restore original pre-fix file before further use

pass_after.command: flock /tmp/aether-pytest.lock bash scripts/run-tests.sh -q -p no:xdist -o addopts="" tests/test_cover006_camelcase.py (in wt-after, unmodified checkout of 9e7befb)

checks.verdict: MATCHES fixer's claimed counts exactly (11 failed/87 passed before, 98/98 after)

checks.method: Independently selected 7 cover-letter/clstudio-adjacent test files (own selection, overlapping but not identical to the fixer's 12-file batch) run against wt-after (9e7befb) via scripts/run-tests.sh under the same flock.
checks.note: Ran as a backgrounded shell task; output file located and read directly after the harness's completion-notification mechanism did not fire (known harness issue this run) — command had already finished cleanly with a healthy exit, lock free, no stray pytest process, well before this was noticed.

checks.method: Own differential harness (/tmp/review-nf-final-pass-001/differential/diff_test.py, not derived from the fixer's characterization table): the retired _CAMEL_HUMP_RE extracted verbatim via `git show f491170:...` and the new _camel_humps extracted verbatim via `git show 9e7befb:...`, run head-to-head over 895 unique generated ASCII tokens (single chars, all 2/3-char class combinations of {upper,lower,digit}, acronym runs of length 1-6 with 0-4 trailing lowercase/digit tails, chained multi-hump tokens, leading-lowercase fragments, trailing-acronym tokens, digits interleaved with camel runs, 300 pure-random alnum strings length 0-15, empty string).
checks.result: 0 mismatches / 895 tokens — byte-identical hump segmentation on every ASCII input tested, beyond the fixer's 37-case characterization table
checks.verdict: Confirms fixer's claim (b): ASCII/acronym behavior is byte-identical across the rewrite, independently, at fuzz scale

checks.method: Targeted probes beyond the fixer's 5 named scripts: German eszett (ß, islower()=True/isupper()=False, no single-codepoint uppercase form), a titlecase character (ǅ 'DZ with caron', isupper()=False/istitle()=True), a combining acute mark (U+0301) immediately after a capital, NFC vs NFD forms of an accented capital (É), and digits inside/adjacent to non-Latin (Cyrillic) runs.

divergence_detail.materiality: Old regex truncated 'Straße' to 'Stra' because ß failed [a-z0-9]; new correctly keeps the whole German word as one segment. Does NOT change the artifact verdict either way (see C5) — net improvement, not a regression.
Precomposed accented capital now starts a hump (in-scope, same class as the München/Istanbul fix).
Digits inside/around a Cyrillic run correctly absorbed into the hump; in-scope.
Standalone (unglued) term, single segment either way — no artifact-verdict effect.

titlecase_gap_found.description: ǅ (U+01C5, Unicode category Lt 'titlecase') is NOT recognized as a hump start by _camel_humps (ch.isupper() is False for Lt characters) — identical non-detection to the old regex. A token like 'ǅLocation' still produces only ['Location'] post-fix, same as pre-fix, so a hypothetical proper noun spelled with a precomposed Croatian/Slavic digraph titlecase codepoint (Dž/Lj/Nj family) glued to a JD label would NOT be caught by this fix.
titlecase_gap_found.materiality_judgment: NOTE, not a FAIL. Category-Lt precomposed digraphs are vanishingly rare in real-world text — virtually all real documents (including scraped JDs) spell these as two separate characters ('Dž', not 'ǅ'), and no reproduction of this shape exists in the finding's reported cases or in production evidence. The commit's comment claims 'Unicode-correct for every cased script in one shot,' which is a very slight overclaim (Lt is technically a third case category the walk doesn't special-case) — worth a follow-up doc note but does not block this LOW-severity fix, which targets the 5 concretely-reported non-Latin gluings and their structural class (Lu/Ll cased scripts), not the Lt category.

checks.verdict: All segment-level divergences are either (a) in-scope intentional expansions matching the fix's stated purpose, or (b) a pre-existing non-detection gap (titlecase) that is unchanged by the fix and immaterial to real JD text. No regression found.

checks.method: Re-implemented _is_camel_concatenation_artifact's exact logic (allowlist check, len<2 early-return, ARTIFACT_SPLIT_WORDS all-match rule, ARTIFACT_LABEL_WORDS any-match rule — transcribed verbatim from the reviewed file) and ran it, with both old_humps and new _camel_humps as the segmenter, over all 895 ASCII tokens + the 20 unicode probe tokens (915 total).
checks.result: 3 verdict changes, all False→True: ÉclairLocation, Москва2Salary, 2МоскваSalary. Zero True→False changes (no legitimate term was ever newly mis-flagged; no previously-caught artifact stopped being caught).
checks.confirmed_reported_cases: Directly re-ran the exact 10 reported gluings (5 named + 5 reverse-order) and 3 standalone-preserved cases through the same harness: all 10 gluings flip False→True (fix works); all 3 standalone terms (Киев/İstanbul/Zürich) remain False→False (no new false positives on legitimate unglued proper nouns).
checks.verdict: The fix's user-visible behavior change is exactly and only the intended one — no collateral verdict flips anywhere in the tested space

checks.method: grep -rn '_CAMEL_HUMP_RE|_camel_humps' across the entire repo (not just apps/api)
checks.result: _CAMEL_HUMP_RE: 0 occurrences anywhere in the reviewed tree (fully removed, confirming no dangling reference). _camel_humps: defined once at apps/api/app/routers/cover_letters.py, called once at the same file's _is_camel_concatenation_artifact (line ~259), and referenced by name only in a resolver helper (_active_camel_humps) in the test file for the byte-identical characterization suite (not a second production call site).
checks.output_contract: list[str] preserved — confirmed by direct execution (both harnesses above call it and receive Python lists of strings); the sole consumer's logic (len(segments) check, .lower() on each segment) is unchanged in the diff.
checks.verdict: Single call site confirmed; output contract unchanged

checks.method: git diff --stat f491170 9e7befb; git diff f491170 9e7befb -- apps/api/tests/test_cover006_camelcase.py | grep '^-' (deletion-line count); grep -niE 'TODO|FIXME|type:\s*ignore|noqa|except:|placeholder|mock|Math\.random|--no-verify|eslint-disable|@ts-ignore' on the full commit diff
checks.diff_stat: apps/api/app/routers/cover_letters.py | 73 +++++++++-  ;  apps/api/tests/test_cover006_camelcase.py | 233 ++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++  ;  2 files changed, 303 insertions(+), 3 deletions(-)
checks.test_file_deletions: 0 actual content deletions (the single '^-' match is the diff header line 'a/apps/api/tests/test_cover006_camelcase.py' itself) — confirmed purely additive, no existing test modified or removed
checks.prod_file_deletions: 3 lines removed are exactly the old _CAMEL_HUMP_RE constant + its one-line docstring reference + the old .findall() call site line, replaced by the new function and call
checks.commit_message_accuracy: Verified against the actual diff and actual test run counts — accurate, no overclaims beyond the minor Lt/titlecase nuance noted in C4
checks.no_no_verify: Confirmed via git log — ordinary commit, no bypass flags evident
checks.verdict: Scope is exactly the two files claimed; no prohibited patterns; commit message accurate

checks.method: Read all 233 added lines of the test file. The 5 new test functions covering the 10 reported gluings + reverse-order variants, the 3 standalone-preserved cases, and the end-to-end keyword-panel repro all call the real _is_camel_concatenation_artifact / _keyword_coverage functions with literal input strings and assert on their real boolean/dict outputs — not tautological. The ASCII characterization table's 37 (token, expected_segments) pairs are hard-coded literal expected values (not computed by calling the function under test), so the assertion `got == expected` is a genuine regression guard, not circular. The one structural device worth flagging — _active_camel_humps() resolving to whichever segmenter is wired in — is legitimate: it lets the identical characterization file run unmodified pre- and post-fix (which is how this reviewer independently reproduced the fixer's fail-before/pass-after numbers using the SAME test file against BOTH SHAs), not a mechanism that could mask a bug.
checks.verdict: Tests assert real, fixed behavior; no tautologies found

differential_audit_summary.conclusion: No ASCII regression at fuzz scale; every non-ASCII divergence found is either in-scope (matches the fix's stated purpose) or an unchanged pre-existing non-issue (titlecase Lt category, immaterial to real JD text).

reasoning: The fix is minimal (single-function replacement plus one call-site update, 3 production lines removed / ~35 added incl. extensive comments, additive-only test file), addresses the verified root cause (ASCII-only [A-Z] hump-start test in the retired regex), and is independently reproducible: fail-before (11/87) and pass-after (98/98) counts match exactly on fresh execution; a 895-token adversarial ASCII fuzz found zero divergence from the retired regex (exceeding the fixer's 37-case characterization table by >20x); a verdict-level differential over the full ASCII+unicode probe set found exactly 3 changes, all intentional False→True expansions matching the reported defect class, and zero True→False regressions; the single unicode gap found (Unicode category-Lt titlecase characters, e.g. ǅ) is a pre-existing non-issue unchanged by the fix and not reproducible in real job-description text, so it is a documentation note rather than a review blocker. Scope is exactly cover_letters.py + its test file per grep confirmation of the single call site and unchanged list[str] output contract; no prohibited patterns (no TODO/mock/placeholder, no suppressed errors, no eslint/ts-ignore equivalents, no --no-verify) were found in the diff; commit message is accurate. No regression was found in an independently-selected 7-file/95-test batch of adjacent cover-letter suites.
